tasks.description: Pre-flight the runpod-lifecycle package at /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/runpod-lifecycle. Read src/runpod_lifecycle/__init__.py and enumerate public exports (confirm/record presence of RunPodConfig, launch, terminate, list_pods, find_orphans, get_network_volumes, api.{create_pod,get_pod_status,get_pod_ssh_details}, and an SSH helper if present — and record which of these are async def vs sync). Read pyproject.toml to confirm name='runpod-lifecycle', version='0.1.0', and that a dev extra or dev deps cover pytest/pytest-asyncio/pytest-mock. Ensure .gitignore excludes .venv/, .pytest_cache/, __pycache__/, *.pyc, .env*, *.egg-info/ (create or extend if missing). Run `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/runpod-lifecycle && .venv/bin/python -m pytest -q` and require ≥50 tests passed as the gate. If ≥50 passed does not hold, STOP and surface to the user — do not proceed to publish.

Publish runpod-lifecycle v0.1.0 to github.com/banodoco/runpod-lifecycle. Working dir: /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/runpod-lifecycle. Steps: (1) `git init -b main` if not already initialized. (2) Stage source only: src/, tests/, pyproject.toml, README.md, .gitignore, LICENSE if present — verify via `git status` and `git diff --cached --stat` that NO .venv/, .pytest_cache/, .env*, *.egg-info/, or __pycache__ are staged. (3) Commit with message 'Initial commit: runpod_lifecycle v0.1.0'. (4) Run `gh repo create banodoco/runpod-lifecycle --public --source=. --push --description 'Reusable async RunPod lifecycle, discovery, and CLI for the Banodoco stack.'`. (5) `git tag v0.1.0 && git push origin v0.1.0`. (6) Verify with `gh repo view banodoco/runpod-lifecycle --json url,isPrivate,description` that it is public and description matches, and `gh api repos/banodoco/runpod-lifecycle/tags` shows v0.1.0. This repo MAY be pushed to main directly (no prior history); consumer repos MAY NOT.

Create orchestrator migration branch and wire the new dependency. Working dir: /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-worker-orchestrator. Confirm `git status` is clean, then `git checkout -b runpod-lifecycle-migration` from main. Add the line `runpod-lifecycle @ git+https://github.com/banodoco/runpod-lifecycle@v0.1.0` to gpu_orchestrator/requirements.txt (do NOT assume a root pyproject.toml — this repo doesn't have one). Install into the orchestrator's venv with `pip install -r gpu_orchestrator/requirements.txt` and verify import works: `python -c 'import runpod_lifecycle; print(runpod_lifecycle.__version__)'`. Do NOT use `pip install -e .[dev]`.

Audit the async/sync boundary in gpu_orchestrator/control_loop.py. Read the file at the observed self.runpod.* call-site line numbers (approximate: 386, 448, 465, 1126, 1205, 1301, 1306, 1336, 1342, 1480, 1502, 1517, 1541, 1544, 1557, 1598, 1600, 1651). For each, determine whether the enclosing def is `async def` (expected: most — evidence is that db.update_worker_status is AsyncMock in tests). Grep the scripts in scripts/ to confirm they are sync-shaped entry points (top-level `if __name__ == '__main__'` with no active loop). Record the audit result as a brief top-of-file comment in the new adapter file (to be created in T5) so the async contract is explicit. Output findings (enclosing-function sync/async tally, scripts that will need asyncio.run wrappers) in executor_notes for use by T5–T7.

Create gpu_orchestrator/worker_spawner.py — the WorkerSpawnerAdapter translation layer. All public methods that call into runpod_lifecycle MUST be `async def`; the only sync method is the pure-string `generate_worker_id`. Required public surface: async spawn_worker(worker_id, worker_env=None) -> Optional[Dict]; async start_worker_process(runpod_id, worker_id, has_pending_tasks=False) -> bool; async check_and_initialize_worker(worker_id, runpod_id) -> Dict; async terminate_worker(runpod_id) -> bool; async get_pod_status(runpod_id) -> Dict; async execute_command_on_worker(runpod_id, cmd, ...) -> SSHResult; generate_worker_id() -> str (sync, copy verbatim from current RunpodClient); async check_storage_health(storage_name) -> Dict; async get_storage_volume_id(storage_name) -> Optional[str]; async expand_network_volume(volume_id, new_size) -> bool. spawn_worker must import and call gpu_orchestrator.runpod.startup_script (the preserved module) to build the startup script; must preserve RAM-tier fallback, storage-volume fallback, and startup-script injection behavior; then `await runpod_lifecycle.launch(...)`. terminate_worker is a thin `await runpod_lifecycle.terminate(runpod_id, api_key)`. get_pod_status delegates to `await runpod_lifecycle.api.get_pod_status(runpod_id, api_key)`. Storage helpers: if runpod_lifecycle exports equivalents, delegate; else inline using runpod_lifecycle.get_network_volumes + direct RunPod SDK as fallback (and flag in executor_notes for potential T10 package re-export). Constructor signature: (config, db, runpod_config: RunPodConfig). db is stored but MUST NOT be used for any DB write/insert inside any adapter method. Factory: create_worker_spawner(config, db) -> WorkerSpawnerAdapter that internally calls RunPodConfig.from_env() — drop-in replacement for the old create_runpod_client(). Cap the file at ~400 lines; no opportunistic refactors; no unrelated functionality. Add a top-of-file comment documenting the async contract and the no-Supabase-writes constraint. Run `python -m py_compile gpu_orchestrator/worker_spawner.py` as a syntax gate.

Swap orchestrator consumer scripts to the new adapter or bare package as appropriate. Working dir: /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-worker-orchestrator. Group A — scripts that need client-level behavior (spawn_worker, generate_worker_id, execute_command_on_worker, storage privates) must import `from gpu_orchestrator.worker_spawner import create_worker_spawner` and wrap async adapter calls with `asyncio.run(...)`: scripts/spawn_gpu.py:16, scripts/ssh_to_worker.py:14, scripts/shutdown_all_workers.py:27, scripts/terminate_single_worker.py:21, scripts/test_runpod.py:15, scripts/spot_instance_lifetime_test.py:25,109 (direct runpod_lifecycle.get_network_volumes OK at :109 for volume listing), scripts/debug/commands/storage.py:151 (adapter for storage helpers; direct runpod_lifecycle.get_network_volumes where applicable), scripts/debug/services/workers.py:10. Group B — scripts using only module-level helpers migrate directly to runpod_lifecycle: scripts/uv_e2e_test.py:16-18 and scripts/uv_lock_benchmark.py:22-24 — use `from runpod_lifecycle.api import create_pod, get_pod_ssh_details` and (if exported) `from runpod_lifecycle.ssh import SSHClient`, wrapping with asyncio.run(...). scripts/debug/commands/runpod.py:14 — if raw `runpod` SDK is used for list/orphans, migrate to runpod_lifecycle.list_pods / find_orphans; otherwise leave. Mapping reference: create_runpod_client() → create_worker_spawner(config, db) OR RunPodConfig.from_env() depending on need; terminate_worker(pod_id) → await runpod_lifecycle.terminate(pod_id, api_key) or await spawner.terminate_worker(pod_id); get_pod_status(pod_id) → await runpod_lifecycle.api.get_pod_status(pod_id, api_key); create_pod_and_wait → await runpod_lifecycle.api.create_pod; get_pod_ssh_details → await runpod_lifecycle.api.get_pod_ssh_details; get_network_volumes → await runpod_lifecycle.get_network_volumes. Run `python -m py_compile` on each edited file as syntax gate. If an import gap (e.g., missing SSHClient) is discovered, record in executor_notes for T10 decision.

Wire the new adapter into gpu_orchestrator/control_loop.py. At control_loop.py:44 replace `self.runpod = create_runpod_client()` with `self.runpod = create_worker_spawner(self.config, self.db)`. Add `await` to every self.runpod.* call site (all 23 observed sites including 386, 448, 465, 1126, 1205, 1301, 1306, 1336, 1342, 1480, 1502, 1517, 1541, 1544, 1557, 1598, 1600, 1651) EXCEPT generate_worker_id (sync). DO NOT move or duplicate the Supabase write flow: create_worker_record() before spawn_worker and update_worker_status() after spawn/terminate MUST remain in control_loop.py, not in the adapter. Preserve RAM-tier fallback, storage-volume fallback, and startup-script injection — these stay structurally identical inside the adapter's spawn_worker. No opportunistic refactoring. After edits, grep the repo for `from gpu_orchestrator.runpod import` and `from gpu_orchestrator.runpod.` and update every non-startup_script hit to the new paths. Run `python -m py_compile gpu_orchestrator/control_loop.py`.

Delete obsolete orchestrator code and fix remaining tests. In /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-worker-orchestrator: delete gpu_orchestrator/runpod/api.py, client.py, lifecycle.py, storage.py, ssh.py. KEEP gpu_orchestrator/runpod/startup_script.py. Trim gpu_orchestrator/runpod/__init__.py to re-export only from startup_script. Delete obsolete tests: tests/test_runpod_decomposition.py (~168 lines), tests/test_direct_runpod_lifecycle.py, tests/test_direct_runpod_storage.py, tests/gpu_orchestrator/runpod/test_lifecycle.py, test_storage.py, test_api.py, test_client.py. KEEP tests/gpu_orchestrator/runpod/test_startup_script.py (~89 real lines — tests the surviving module). Update tests/gpu_orchestrator/control/phases/test_lifecycle.py:34,48 so that `host.runpod = Mock(spec=WorkerSpawnerAdapter)`; every async adapter method being mocked (spawn_worker, terminate_worker, get_pod_status, start_worker_process, check_and_initialize_worker, execute_command_on_worker, check_storage_health, get_storage_volume_id, expand_network_volume) must use AsyncMock; keep generate_worker_id as a plain sync Mock. Grep the orchestrator for residual `create_runpod_client` and `RunpodClient` references — expect zero outside of lines being deleted. Grep for any remaining `gpu_orchestrator.runpod.{api,client,lifecycle,storage,ssh}` imports — expect zero.

Verify orchestrator tests are green and push the branch. `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-worker-orchestrator && pytest -q tests/` — must exit 0 with no 'coroutine was never awaited' warnings and no AttributeError. If anything fails, fix the consumer code (control_loop.py, adapter, scripts, tests) — NOT the runpod_lifecycle package. When green, `git add` only the intended edits (verify no .env, no .venv, no secret keys), commit with a descriptive message, and `git push -u origin runpod-lifecycle-migration`. DO NOT open a PR. DO NOT push to main. Confirm with `git log origin/main..HEAD` that commits target the migration branch only.

CONTINGENT: Publish runpod-lifecycle v0.1.1 if and only if T1/T5/T6 surfaced a missing export needed by consumers (e.g., SSHClient, storage helpers like get_storage_volume_id / check_storage_health / expand_network_volume). Per-symbol decision: (a) re-export in /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/runpod-lifecycle/src/runpod_lifecycle/__init__.py, bump pyproject.toml version to 0.1.1, commit, `git tag v0.1.1 && git push origin main v0.1.1`, then update both consumer requirements/pyproject pins to `@v0.1.1`; OR (b) write a consumer-local shim in gpu_orchestrator/worker_spawner.py (preferred for orchestrator-only idioms like storage-mixin internals). Do NOT add unrelated functionality; do NOT bump version for cosmetic changes. If no gaps were surfaced, record that in executor_notes and SKIP this task (mark status 'skipped'). If gaps were fixed via shim only (option b), also skip the tag bump.

Prepare the reigh-worker migration branch, preserving the dirty tree. Working dir: /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-worker. Run `git status --short` and record the pre-migration dirty file set in executor_notes. Compare against the migration target set: scripts/live_test/config.py, scripts/live_test/variant_fresh.py, scripts/live_test/variant_update.py, debug/commands/storage.py, debug/commands/runpod.py, scripts/live_test/__init__.py, scripts/live_test/ssh_bootstrap.py, scripts/live_test/terminate_guard.py, scripts/live_test/tests/test_primitives.py, pyproject.toml. If ANY overlap, STOP and surface to the user for resolution (do not auto-merge). If zero overlap: `git stash push -m 'pre-migration-unrelated-changes'`, `git checkout -b runpod-lifecycle-migration` from main, `git stash pop`. Add `runpod-lifecycle @ git+https://github.com/banodoco/runpod-lifecycle@v0.1.0` (or @v0.1.1 if T10 tagged one) to pyproject.toml under project dependencies — DO NOT introduce a [dev] extra. Install with `pip install -e .` plus whatever existing test-dep file reigh-worker uses (confirm its location before running; do NOT invent `pip install -e .[dev]`). Verify with `python -c 'import runpod_lifecycle'`.

Rewrite reigh-worker call sites to runpod_lifecycle. Working dir: /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-worker. Brief-listed files: (a) scripts/live_test/config.py:73 — RunpodClient construction → RunPodConfig.from_env() (with adapter imports only if surrounding code needs client-level behavior). (b) scripts/live_test/variant_fresh.py and scripts/live_test/variant_update.py — low-level calls → runpod_lifecycle.api.*, awaited via asyncio.run(...) at entry points. (c) debug/commands/storage.py:18 — create_runpod_client → RunPodConfig.from_env() + await runpod_lifecycle.get_network_volumes(...). (d) debug/commands/runpod.py:14,30 — raw `runpod` SDK → await runpod_lifecycle.list_pods / find_orphans. Critique-surfaced files (FLAG-004): (e) scripts/live_test/__init__.py — lazy imports of gpu_orchestrator.runpod.{lifecycle,ssh,api} will break once T8 deletes those modules. Rewrite to import from runpod_lifecycle directly (or inline the shim the module was lazily reaching for). (f) scripts/live_test/ssh_bootstrap.py — replace gpu_orchestrator.runpod.ssh imports with runpod_lifecycle.ssh equivalents. (g) scripts/live_test/terminate_guard.py — replace gpu_orchestrator.runpod.* usage with await runpod_lifecycle.terminate / api.get_pod_status. (h) scripts/live_test/tests/test_primitives.py — retarget monkeypatches: `monkeypatch.setattr('gpu_orchestrator.runpod.client', ...)` → the new path (likely runpod_lifecycle.lifecycle or the local live_test_pkg shim). Keep live_test_pkg.terminate_pod monkeypatch if that symbol still exists; otherwise update to the new attribute. Run `python -m py_compile` on each edited file. Final grep gate: `grep -r 'gpu_orchestrator.runpod' --include='*.py' .` must return zero hits.

Verify reigh-worker tests and push branch. `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-worker && pytest -q` — must exit 0. Fix consumer code on any failure, NOT the package. Confirm no changes to the pre-migration dirty file set by re-running `git status --short` and diffing against the set recorded in T11. If the dirty set was stashed-then-popped in T11 and conflicts occurred, resolve by preserving both the unrelated changes AND the migration edits before committing. Commit only the migration edits (verify with `git diff --cached` no secrets, no .env, no unintended files). `git push -u origin runpod-lifecycle-migration`. DO NOT open a PR. DO NOT push to main.

Final cross-repo verification gate. Re-run each repo's tests and confirm all invariants. (1) `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/runpod-lifecycle && .venv/bin/python -m pytest -q` — ≥50 passed, exit 0. (2) `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-worker-orchestrator && pytest -q tests/` — exit 0, no 'coroutine was never awaited' warnings. (3) `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-worker && pytest -q` — exit 0. (4) In the orchestrator repo, grep `grep -r 'from gpu_orchestrator.runpod' --include='*.py'` — expect only startup_script hits. (5) In reigh-worker, grep `grep -r 'gpu_orchestrator.runpod' --include='*.py'` — expect zero hits. (6) In both consumer repos, grep `create_runpod_client` and `RunpodClient` — expect zero hits. (7) `gh repo view banodoco/runpod-lifecycle` confirms public + description; `gh api repos/banodoco/runpod-lifecycle/tags` shows v0.1.0 (and v0.1.1 if T10 fired). (8) Confirm orchestrator's gpu_orchestrator/requirements.txt and reigh-worker's pyproject.toml pin the intended tag. (9) `git log origin/main..HEAD` on both consumer repos confirms commits are ONLY on runpod-lifecycle-migration, nothing on main. (10) Spot-check with `git log -p --all | grep -iE 'RUNPOD_API_KEY|SUPABASE_SERVICE|sk-' | head` confirms no secrets in any committed diff. Additionally, write a throwaway reproduction script that imports runpod_lifecycle, constructs RunPodConfig.from_env() (OK to skip if no env available — just stub), instantiates the orchestrator's WorkerSpawnerAdapter with a Mock db, and verifies the method signatures match the expected async contract (e.g., inspect.iscoroutinefunction(adapter.spawn_worker) is True, inspect.iscoroutinefunction(adapter.generate_worker_id) is False). Run the script, confirm PASS, then delete it. If any step fails, read the error, fix the code, and re-run until clean.

watch_items: FLAG-001 (async boundary): runpod_lifecycle.launch/terminate/list_pods/find_orphans are async; adapter methods must be `async def`; every self.runpod.* call site in control_loop.py needs `await` (except sync generate_worker_id); scripts wrap with asyncio.run(...). Watch for 'coroutine was never awaited' RuntimeWarnings in pytest output — they are silent failures.

FLAG-002 (client-behavior scripts): scripts/spawn_gpu.py, ssh_to_worker.py, shutdown_all_workers.py, terminate_single_worker.py, test_runpod.py, spot_instance_lifetime_test.py, debug/commands/storage.py, debug/services/workers.py must migrate to create_worker_spawner adapter, NOT bare runpod_lifecycle, because they depend on spawn_worker/generate_worker_id/execute_command_on_worker/storage privates that the bare package does not expose with orchestrator-specific logic.

FLAG-003 (Supabase ownership): WorkerSpawnerAdapter is a pure translation layer — it MUST NOT call create_worker_record or update_worker_status. control_loop.py keeps the existing db-write bracket around spawn_worker. Duplicate writes would corrupt worker state tracking.

FLAG-004 (reigh-worker cross-imports): beyond the brief's 5 files, scripts/live_test/__init__.py (lazy gpu_orchestrator.runpod.{lifecycle,ssh,api} imports), scripts/live_test/ssh_bootstrap.py, scripts/live_test/terminate_guard.py, and scripts/live_test/tests/test_primitives.py (monkeypatches) all reference gpu_orchestrator.runpod.* and will break after T8 deletions if not retargeted in T12.

FLAG-005 (dep files): orchestrator uses gpu_orchestrator/requirements.txt (no root pyproject). reigh-worker uses pyproject.toml WITHOUT a [dev] extra. Do NOT run `pip install -e .[dev]` on either — it will fail.

Preserve orchestrator fallback behavior: RAM-tier fallback, storage-volume fallback, and startup-script injection from gpu_orchestrator/runpod/startup_script.py (that file STAYS). Adapter's spawn_worker must keep these structurally identical — they are tested live.

gpu_orchestrator/runpod/startup_script.py MUST stay in-place; only api.py, client.py, lifecycle.py, storage.py, ssh.py get deleted. tests/gpu_orchestrator/runpod/test_startup_script.py MUST be kept (tests the surviving module) — excluded from the bulk test delete.
Never push to main on reigh-worker or reigh-worker-orchestrator — branch `runpod-lifecycle-migration` only; let the human open PRs. runpod-lifecycle itself CAN push to main (no prior history).
No secrets in any commit: orchestrator's .env has live RunPod keys; never stage .env*, .venv/, .pytest_cache/, __pycache__/. Verify with `git diff --cached` before every commit.

reigh-worker has unrelated uncommitted changes — MUST be preserved. T11 stash/branch/pop pattern; if any uncommitted file overlaps with the migration target set, STOP and surface to the user rather than merging automatically.

Open question from plan metadata: does runpod_lifecycle actually export SSHClient, storage helpers (get_storage_volume_id, check_storage_health, expand_network_volume)? Gaps trigger T10 (v0.1.1 re-export) or consumer shim. T1 must confirm exports before T5 so the adapter knows what to delegate vs inline.

Open question: how does reigh-worker currently import gpu_orchestrator.runpod.* — editable install, sys.path hack, or sibling-checkout? The retarget path in tests/test_primitives.py monkeypatches depends on this.
Adapter file cap ~400 lines, no unrelated refactors — the cap exists because a swollen translation layer re-creates the coupling the migration is meant to break.

Debt watch [control-decomposition]: two duplicated paths exist (legacy scaling in scaling_decision.py:70 and failsafe in periodic.py:175-229). This plan does NOT address them; ensure the migration does not WORSEN the duplication by adding adapter calls inside those legacy paths without flagging to the user.

Debt watch [failsafe-health-verdict]: failsafe still computes age > cutoff locally rather than using a precomputed is_failsafe_stale verdict. Do not expand this pattern during the migration; if async conversion touches the failsafe path, keep the age calc untouched.

Verifiability-0/1 (minor open flags): success criteria #17 (subjective ~400-line cap) and #18 (live spawn/ready/terminate device test) need human verification. Record a note in T14 if the physical smoke is deferred.
If gh CLI is not authenticated to the banodoco org, T2 will fail at `gh repo create`. Verify `gh auth status` before running. Do not attempt to re-authenticate without surfacing to the user.
Syntax gate: `python -m py_compile` each edited file as you change it — catches import typos instantly, before pytest runs.

sense_checks.question: Did T1 record the actual exported symbols from runpod_lifecycle/__init__.py (with each symbol's async/sync status), confirm pyproject version is 0.1.0, ensure .gitignore excludes .venv/.pytest_cache/__pycache__/.env*/egg-info, and run `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q` with ≥50 tests passing as a gate? Were any missing exports (e.g., SSHClient, storage helpers) flagged for T10 evaluation?

Does `gh repo view banodoco/runpod-lifecycle --json url,isPrivate,description` show public=true and the exact description from the brief? Does `gh api repos/banodoco/runpod-lifecycle/tags` list v0.1.0? Verify `git diff --cached` from the initial commit contained NO .venv/, .pytest_cache/, .env*, __pycache__, or *.egg-info entries.

Is gpu_orchestrator/requirements.txt updated with `runpod-lifecycle @ git+https://github.com/banodoco/runpod-lifecycle@v0.1.0`, does `python -c 'import runpod_lifecycle; print(runpod_lifecycle.__version__)'` succeed, and is HEAD on a freshly-created `runpod-lifecycle-migration` branch (not main)? Confirm no root pyproject.toml was created.

Does the audit confirm which control_loop.py functions enclosing self.runpod.* calls are async def vs sync, and does it list every script that will need asyncio.run() wrapping? Were all 23 observed call-site line numbers cross-checked and any drift from the audited line numbers recorded?

Does gpu_orchestrator/worker_spawner.py expose exactly the required public API (spawn_worker, start_worker_process, check_and_initialize_worker, terminate_worker, get_pod_status, execute_command_on_worker, generate_worker_id, check_storage_health, get_storage_volume_id, expand_network_volume) with all of them async EXCEPT generate_worker_id? Is the no-Supabase-writes constraint written into a top-of-file comment? Does spawn_worker still use the preserved gpu_orchestrator.runpod.startup_script module, keep RAM-tier fallback, and keep storage-volume fallback? Is the file ≤~400 lines with no unrelated refactors?

For each of the 8 client-behavior scripts, does the import now come from gpu_orchestrator.worker_spawner (create_worker_spawner) rather than raw runpod_lifecycle, and are adapter calls wrapped with asyncio.run(...)? For the 2 helper-only scripts (uv_e2e_test.py, uv_lock_benchmark.py), are imports pulled from runpod_lifecycle.api / runpod_lifecycle.ssh? Does `python -m py_compile` succeed on every edited file?

Does control_loop.py:44 now construct self.runpod via create_worker_spawner(self.config, self.db)? Are all 23 self.runpod.* call sites awaited EXCEPT generate_worker_id? Is the existing create_worker_record → spawn_worker → update_worker_status bracket still in control_loop.py (NOT duplicated into the adapter)? Does a grep for `from gpu_orchestrator.runpod import` (excluding startup_script) return zero?

Have gpu_orchestrator/runpod/{api,client,lifecycle,storage,ssh}.py been deleted and startup_script.py retained? Has gpu_orchestrator/runpod/__init__.py been trimmed to re-export only from startup_script? Have the obsolete tests been deleted and tests/gpu_orchestrator/runpod/test_startup_script.py been KEPT? Does test_lifecycle.py now use Mock(spec=WorkerSpawnerAdapter) with AsyncMock for async methods and plain Mock for generate_worker_id? Does a repo grep for `create_runpod_client` and `RunpodClient` return zero hits?

Did `pytest -q tests/` pass green with no AttributeError and no 'coroutine was never awaited' warnings? Does `git log origin/main..HEAD` confirm commits are on runpod-lifecycle-migration only (nothing on main)? Does `git diff main...HEAD --stat` show no .env, no .venv, no secret keys? Was `git push -u origin runpod-lifecycle-migration` completed with no PR opened?

If package exports were complete (no SSHClient/storage-helper gaps), was T10 correctly skipped and recorded as such? If gaps existed, was the per-symbol decision (re-export+v0.1.1 vs consumer shim) documented, and did the chosen path result in a working consumer install without introducing unrelated functionality? If v0.1.1 was tagged, were both consumer deps updated to pin @v0.1.1?

Did T11 record the pre-migration `git status --short` output and verify zero overlap with the migration target set before branching? Was the stash/branch/pop pattern executed cleanly? Is the new git dep (@v0.1.0 or @v0.1.1) in pyproject.toml project dependencies (not in a synthetic [dev] extra)? Does `python -c 'import runpod_lifecycle'` succeed?

Are all 9 target files (config.py:73, variant_fresh.py, variant_update.py, debug/commands/storage.py:18, debug/commands/runpod.py:14,30, scripts/live_test/__init__.py, ssh_bootstrap.py, terminate_guard.py, tests/test_primitives.py) migrated with adapter imports or runpod_lifecycle imports as appropriate? Are the monkeypatches in test_primitives.py retargeted to the new paths (no stale gpu_orchestrator.runpod.client references)? Does `grep -r 'gpu_orchestrator.runpod' --include='*.py' .` return zero? Does `python -m py_compile` succeed on every edited file?

Did reigh-worker's `pytest -q` pass green? Does `git status --short` after the commit show the same unrelated dirty files as recorded in T11 (i.e., the pre-migration uncommitted changes were preserved)? Is HEAD on runpod-lifecycle-migration (not main) and was the push to that branch successful with no PR opened?

Do all three pytest runs exit 0 (runpod-lifecycle ≥50 passed, orchestrator green, reigh-worker green)? Do all grep gates return their expected results (orchestrator: only startup_script hits; reigh-worker: zero gpu_orchestrator.runpod hits; both: zero create_runpod_client/RunpodClient hits)? Does `git log origin/main..HEAD` on both consumer repos confirm nothing was pushed to main? Does `gh repo view banodoco/runpod-lifecycle` show public+v0.1.0 tag? Does the throwaway async-contract reproduction script confirm inspect.iscoroutinefunction on adapter methods matches the contract, and was the script deleted after running?

meta_commentary: Execution notes for this finalize: (1) The three repos are independent git trees in the same workspace — do NOT confuse working directories. Use absolute paths in cd commands. (2) Phase 1 (T1-T2) gates everything: if the package tests don't hit ≥50, or the gh push fails, STOP and surface rather than patching around it. (3) T4's audit is CHEAP but LOAD-BEARING — get it right once so T5, T7, and all scripts can be written with correct async/await placement; rushing past T4 will manifest as silent 'coroutine was never awaited' warnings that tests may not fail on. (4) The critical invariant for T5 is that the adapter is a PURE TRANSLATION LAYER — no Supabase writes, no env reads inside methods, no orchestrator-specific control-flow logic (scaling, failsafe). If you find yourself reimplementing control_loop logic in the adapter, stop — you've drifted. (5) The ~400-line cap on worker_spawner.py is subjective but directional: a swollen adapter re-creates the coupling the migration breaks. (6) T8's deletion set is exact — KEEP startup_script.py and test_startup_script.py; DELETE the other five implementation modules and their corresponding tests. A mis-deletion here takes out the live-tested fallback path. (7) T10 is contingent on gaps surfaced in T1/T5/T6. If you discover a missing export LATE (e.g., during T12 on reigh-worker), you may either retroactively tag v0.1.1 and update both consumers, or add a reigh-worker-local shim — prefer the shim for one-off reigh-worker concerns, the re-export for orchestrator needs. (8) T11's stash/branch/pop is the CAREFUL path; if overlap exists between dirty files and migration targets, STOP — do not merge. Auto-merging someone else's uncommitted WIP with a migration is how work gets lost. (9) T12's grep gate (`grep -r 'gpu_orchestrator.runpod'`) is the definitive check that reigh-worker no longer depends on the deleted orchestrator submodule — a hit here means Phase 2's deletion will crash reigh-worker at import time. (10) T14's throwaway script is a cheap async-contract check that pytest may miss if mocks mask the boundary; write it, run it, verify signatures, delete it. (11) Autonomous non-destructive actions (branch creation, commits on migration branches, push to migration branches, tag push on runpod-lifecycle which has no history) are pre-authorized per the idea brief. Opening PRs and pushing to main on consumer repos are NOT authorized — stop at push. (12) Debt watch items are defensive: do not let the async conversion worsen the existing legacy-scaling/failsafe duplication — if T7 touches those paths, keep the behavior identical. (13) Baseline tests established before changes: runpod-lifecycle should have ≥50 passing (T1 gate confirms); reigh-worker-orchestrator pytest -q tests/ should pass on clean main; reigh-worker pytest -q status is unknown pre-migration due to the uncommitted tree — note this in executor_notes for T11 if the pre-migration pytest surfaces failures unrelated to the migration.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Phase 1 Step 1 — Pre-flight the runpod-lifecycle package (read exports, check pyproject, gitignore, run pytest ≥50)
Phase 1 Step 2 — Initial commit + publish to banodoco/runpod-lifecycle, tag v0.1.0, verify
Phase 2 Step 3 — Create migration branch + add runpod-lifecycle dep to gpu_orchestrator/requirements.txt + install
Phase 2 Step 4 — Audit async/sync boundary in control_loop.py and scripts
Phase 2 Step 5 — Build WorkerSpawnerAdapter at gpu_orchestrator/worker_spawner.py with full async surface, no DB writes, preserved fallbacks
Phase 2 Step 6 — Migrate orchestrator consumer scripts (Group A to adapter, Group B direct to runpod_lifecycle)
Phase 2 Step 7 — Wire adapter into control_loop.py with await on all 23 call sites, preserve Supabase bracket

Phase 2 Step 8 — Delete gpu_orchestrator/runpod/{api,client,lifecycle,storage,ssh}.py + obsolete tests; keep startup_script.py + test_startup_script.py; update test_lifecycle.py with Mock(spec=WorkerSpawnerAdapter)+AsyncMock
Phase 2 Step 9 — Run orchestrator pytest green, commit, push migration branch (no PR, no main push)
Phase 2 Step 10 (contingent) — Tag runpod-lifecycle v0.1.1 with re-exports OR add consumer shim, if gaps surfaced
Phase 3 Step 11 — Branch reigh-worker preserving dirty tree (stash/branch/pop), add dep to pyproject.toml, install
Phase 3 Step 12 — Rewrite reigh-worker call sites (brief-listed 5 files + critique-surfaced 4 files), grep gate for zero gpu_orchestrator.runpod hits
Phase 3 Step 13 — Run reigh-worker pytest green, commit, push migration branch, preserve unrelated dirty files

Phase 4 Step 14 — Cross-repo end-to-end verification: all three pytest runs green, grep gates clean, gh repo view confirms v0.1.0, no main pushes, no secrets, throwaway async-contract reproduction script

validation.completeness_notes: All 14 approved-plan steps map to exactly one finalize task each (T1–T14). No orphan tasks. T10 is contingent and may be marked 'skipped' if T1/T5/T6 surface no package export gaps — this mirrors the plan's own 'contingent' label. Debt-watch items are tracked in watch_items rather than creating new tasks, since the plan explicitly does not address them; watch_items flags the 'do no harm' constraint to prevent the migration from worsening existing duplication. The final task T14 is the required tests+verify capstone and includes a throwaway reproduction script for the async contract per the finalize requirements.

baseline_test_note: Baseline tests were not executed during finalize — this is a planning artifact, not an execution run. The three pytest commands listed form the expected pre-migration baseline: (1) runpod-lifecycle should have ≥50 passing (T1 enforces this as a gate before publish), (2) reigh-worker-orchestrator is on clean main and is expected green (T9 re-verifies after migration), (3) reigh-worker has unrelated uncommitted changes and its pre-migration pass/fail state is UNKNOWN — the executor should record the pre-migration pytest result in T11's executor_notes so post-migration comparison in T13 can distinguish migration-caused regressions from pre-existing failures in the dirty tree. T14 is the authoritative post-migration verification gate.
